title: Chennakeshava Temple, Hullekere
tagline: Chennakeshava Temple, Hullekere — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Chennakeshava temple, dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu is located in the village of Hullekere, in the Arasikere Taluk, about 22 km from the commercial town Arasikere.
overview.description: The Chennakeshava temple, dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu is located in the village of Hullekere, in the Arasikere Taluk, about 22 km from the commercial town Arasikere. It was built in 1163 A.D. by a minister of Hoysala empire king Narasimha I. The art historian Adam Hardy categorizes the architectural style as a single shrine (vimana) construction with miniature vimanas, the basic building material being Soap stone. The monument is protected by the Karnataka state division of Archaeological Survey of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chennakeshava Temple, Hullekere?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chennakeshava Temple, Hullekere is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chennakeshava Temple, Hullekere?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (102 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.
